cli.description: Multi-AI model dev team simulation - Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI collaboration

run.description: Run full pipeline (requirements → meeting → development → PR)
run.modeFullAuto: All confirmations auto-approved (stops only on errors)
run.modeSemiAuto: Confirms only on phase transitions, auto-proceeds within phases
run.modeManual: Waits for user input at every confirmation point

meeting.description: Run a meeting only (kickoff or design)

testModels.description: Test connection status of configured models

init.description: Create config file in the current directory
init.step2: 2. Assign models per persona in agent-team.config.yaml

start.description: Start interactive REPL mode (session-persistent, slash commands)
start.optResume: Resume previous session (latest if ID omitted)

cli.optLang: UI language: en, ko, ja, zh-CN

config.githubStep3: 3. Verify the token has access to the repository

agent.systemPrompt:
You are "{{name}}", a {{role}} on a software development team.

## Personality & Traits
{{description}}

## Expertise
{{expertise}}

## Communication Style
{{style}}

## Behavioral Rules
- Always speak from the perspective of "{{name}}" ({{role}})
- Express opinions with confidence in your area of expertise
- Ask questions or express concerns about areas outside your expertise
- Always provide evidence when presenting opinions
- When disagreeing, present alternatives alongside your objections
- Communicate in English

{{context}}
agent.speakNoPrev: Please share your opinion on this topic as {{name}} ({{role}}).
agent.passConditions:
Respond with [PASS] only if any of the following apply:
- You have no tasks to directly undertake on this topic
- The topic has already been sufficiently covered in previous discussions with nothing new to add
- You would be repeating substantially the same content as your previous statement
Do not force content. Only speak when you have new perspectives or specific proposals.
agent.fixCode: Please fix the above code to address the feedback. Write the complete modified code reflecting {{name}}'s ({{role}}) coding style and expertise.
agent.writeCodeNew: Please implement the above task. Write code reflecting {{name}}'s ({{role}}) coding style and expertise.
agent.qaContext: As a QA engineer, verify the code quality and acceptance criteria compliance.
agent.qaInstruction:
Please perform QA verification on the above code.

Response format:
1. Verification results per acceptance criterion (table format)
2. Edge case test results
3. Bugs/issues found
4. Overall verdict: PASS / FAIL

Please include the following JSON block at the end:
```json
{ "verdict": "PASS or FAIL", "summary": "one-line summary" }
```
agent.taskBreakdownContext: The project's technical design is complete. Breaking it down into executable tasks.
agent.taskBreakdownPrompt:
## Project Requirements
{{requirement}}

## Technical Design Decisions
{{designDecisions}}

Please break down the above into tasks.

## Rules
- Each task should be 1-4 hours of work
- Specify the suitable role ({{roles}}) for each task
- Write specific acceptance criteria
- **Specify dependencies conservatively**:
  - List prerequisite task numbers (1-based) as an array. Example: [1] = can start after task 1 completes
  - Only add dependencies when there's actual file/module-level dependency (modifying same file, referencing API interfaces, etc.)
  - Independent tasks use dependencies: [] (candidates for parallel execution)
  - **When in doubt about potential conflicts, add the dependency** (conservative approach)

## Response Format (JSON)
```json
{
  "tasks": [
    {
      "title": "Task title",
      "description": "Detailed description",
      "suitable_role": "backend_dev",
      "estimated_hours": 2,
      "priority": "P0",
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": ["Criterion 1", "Criterion 2"],
      "category": "backend"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Dependency Examples
- DB Schema (Task 1) → API Implementation (Task 2): dependencies: [1] (schema needed before API)
- Frontend Component (Task 3) and Backend API (Task 2) in separate files: dependencies: [] (independent)
- After Task 1 completes, Tasks 2 and 3 can run in parallel: Task 2 dependencies: [1], Task 3 dependencies: [1]
agent.imageNotConfigured: {{name}} ({{role}}) cannot generate images: image_model not configured
agent.generateTitlePrompt: You are responsible for naming projects. Summarize the given requirements into a one-line English title (under 50 characters). Output only the title.
agent.concludeInstruction: Please review the discussion so far. If sufficient conclusions have been reached, start with [CONCLUDE] and summarize the final conclusions and action items. If further discussion is needed, suggest focus points for the next round.
agent.summaryInstruction: Please synthesize the discussion so far and summarize the final conclusions and action items.
agent.designTopic:
Discussing the technical design for project "{{title}}".

## Requirements
{{requirement}}
{{kickoff}}
## Discussion Topics
1. Technology stack selection (language, framework, DB, etc.)
2. System architecture (monolithic vs microservices, etc.)
3. API design direction
4. Frontend structure
5. Deployment strategy
6. Testing strategy

Please provide specific proposals from your area of expertise. If you disagree, rebut and present alternatives.

adapter.codeOnlyInstruction:
Your response must contain only code. If explanations are needed, write them as code comments.
Wrap in markdown code blocks (```).
adapter.reviewInstruction:
Please review the following code.

## Code
```
{{code}}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
{{criteria}}

## Review Format
- Strengths
- Areas for improvement (specify lines/logic)
- Security issues
- Performance issues

Please include the following JSON block at the end:
```json
{ "verdict": "APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED", "summary": "one-line summary" }
```
